problem_statement: LLM-as-a-Judge is increasingly used to evaluate and rank AI-generated code artifacts (generation, repair, test generation) when human review or executable tests are unavailable, but its verdicts are unreliable and prompt-sensitive, threatening the validity of automated oversight in agentic SE pipelines.
insufficiency_evidence: On TestGen the largest swing under GPT saw distraction drop accuracy from 77.46% to 62.51%; generic Qwen3-4B only returns a valid A/B verdict ~44% of inputs (44.37% overall) vs ~99% for Qwen2.5-Coder-3B, frequently generating free-form text until the context limit.
solution_mechanisms: escalate unstable cases to executable evidence or human review
notes: Some OCR/typographic garbling (e.g. 'summaris', 'proviguidance', broken math notation); preprint with placeholder ACM metadata.
